summary: eslint-plus postinstall fetches and executes remote JavaScript via detached child
details: On install, the package's declared postinstall script spawns a detached, stdio-suppressed child process that runs lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js. That file uses axios to GET https://jsonkeeper.com/b/UTUUE and passes the response's `cookie` field into `new Function(require)` for immediate execution with Node's `require` injected — arbitrary remote code executes with full Node privileges on the installer's machine as soon as `npm install` runs. The jsonkeeper.com paste endpoint is a mutable, attacker-controlled host with no pinning, hash, or signature. The postinstall detaches the child and suppresses all stdio to hide the fetch/exec from npm install output. The package additionally impersonates unrelated projects: it is named `eslint-plus` (ESLint-adjacent) but its `main` ships a copy of nodemailer and it claims authorship by the real nodemailer maintainer, with a lookalike homepage — a lure designed to attract installers into running the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
